title: StaleBench: A Benchmark for Answer Freshness in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description:
StaleBench measures answer freshness in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. Most freshness tools check whether the index holds the new document. StaleBench checks the answer: after a fact changes, how long until the system returns the new value, and how often it keeps returning the old one.

It reports two metrics, catch-up latency and stale-answer rate, across three refresh policies (never, batch, immediate), with Wilson 95 percent confidence intervals. Answers are scored by exact match against a controlled ground truth, so no language model acts as a judge. StaleBench works as a black-box tool against any RAG system whose documents can be changed and refreshed.

In experiments across model sizes and retrievers, about 40 to 60 percent of answers stayed stale even with immediate re-indexing. The cause is positional: when both the old and new documents are retrieved, the model tends to follow document order rather than recency. Placing the newest document last reduces the problem.
